question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the FilterField.ESTIMATE extractor (ContractCallRequest::isEstimate returns boolean) which is converted to string 'true'/'false', by crafting requests with estimate=true to match an EQUALS filter on 'true', triggering a REJECT action that causes all gas estimation records to be missing from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the FilterField.GAS extractor which converts the long gas value to a string, and craft a gas value whose string representation contains a substring matching a CONTAINS filter expression, triggering unintended REJECT or THROTTLE actions that cause records to be incorrectly dropped from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the FilterField.VALUE extractor which converts the long value field to a string, by setting value=0 so that String.valueOf(0) = '0' matches a CONTAINS filter expression '0', triggering unintended actions on all zero-value requests and causing their records to be incorrectly handled in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: restore(long gas)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where restore() is called multiple times for the same request (e.g., through exception handling in both ContractController and ContractCallService), causing double-restoration of gas tokens to gasLimitBucket, inflating the bucket beyond capacity and allowing subsequent requests to bypass gas throttling, leading to incorrect records in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that scaleGas() uses Math.floorDiv() which truncates toward negative infinity, so that gas values just below a multiple of GAS_SCALE_FACTOR (e.g., gas=19,999) result in fewer tokens consumed than gas values just above (e.g., gas=20,001), allowing the attacker to maximize request throughput while minimizing gas token consumption and generating more records than the throttle intends?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared global gasLimitBucket (not per-user) by sending a single request with maximum gas (Long.MAX_VALUE) to drain the entire gasLimitBucket, causing all other users' requests to be throttled and their records to be missing from mirror node exports for the remainder of the refill period?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared global rateLimitBucket (not per-user or per-IP) by sending requests at the maximum rate to exhaust all available rate tokens, causing all other users' requests to be rejected with ThrottleException and their records to be missing from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of per-IP or per-account throttling in ThrottleManager to send requests from multiple source IPs or addresses, each consuming rate and gas tokens from the shared global buckets, collectively exhausting the global throttle and causing legitimate single-user records to be missing from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a ContractCallRequest with a very long 'data' field containing a substring that matches a LOG filter expression, causing log.info('{}', request) to write extremely large log entries that overwhelm the logging system, indirectly causing mirror node record export processes to be delayed or records to be missing?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the action() method's LOG case calls log.info('{}', request) which serializes the entire ContractCallRequest, by crafting a request with extremely large field values to cause excessive memory allocation during serialization, potentially causing OOM errors that disrupt mirror node record export pipelines?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the RequestProperties per-filter bucket (createBucket() with capacity=rate) by exhausting the per-filter THROTTLE bucket independently of the global rateLimitBucket, causing requests that pass global throttling to be rejected by the per-filter bucket, resulting in inconsistent throttle enforcement and missing records in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the per-filter bucket in RequestProperties is lazily initialized (@Getter(lazy=true)) and shared across all threads, by sending concurrent requests that race during bucket initialization, potentially causing multiple bucket instances to be created and the THROTTLE rate limit to be applied inconsistently, leading to incorrect records in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: restore(long gas)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a contract execution where the EVM returns gasUsed=0 for a successful transaction, causing gasRemaining = gasLimit - 0 = gasLimit, and Math.min(gasLimit, gasLimitToRestoreBaseline) to restore the full gasLimit back to the bucket, effectively making the request free in terms of gas throttling and allowing unlimited execution records to be generated in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that throttle() does not validate the 'from' address format before passing it to RequestFilter, causing RequestFilter.test() with FilterField.FROM to extract a malformed address string that matches unintended filter expressions, triggering incorrect REJECT or THROTTLE actions and causing records to be incorrectly handled in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the FilterType.CONTAINS case-insensitive matching (Strings.CI::contains) by crafting a 'data' field with Unicode characters that case-fold to match ASCII filter expressions, triggering unintended REJECT actions that cause records to be missing from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that when a REJECT action throws ThrottleException after gasLimitBucket.tryConsume() has already succeeded, the consumed gas tokens are not restored (no restore() call in the REJECT path), causing permanent gas token leakage that depletes the gasLimitBucket and causes legitimate records to be missing from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that when a THROTTLE action throws ThrottleException after gasLimitBucket.tryConsume() has already succeeded, the consumed gas tokens are not restored, causing permanent gas token leakage per throttled request that depletes the gasLimitBucket over time and causes legitimate records to be missing from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sequential filter evaluation in throttle() where multiple RequestProperties are iterated, and a THROTTLE action in the first filter consumes from the per-filter bucket while a REJECT action in the second filter throws ThrottleException, causing the per-filter bucket token to be consumed without the request being processed, leading to incorrect throttle state and missing records?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: restore(long gas)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that restore() calls gasLimitBucket.addTokens() without any upper bound check, so that repeated restore() calls (e.g., via multiple failed requests) can add tokens beyond the bucket's original capacity, causing the gasLimitBucket to hold more tokens than intended and allowing subsequent requests to bypass gas throttling and generate excess records in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the gasLimitBucket capacity is set to scaleGas(gasPerSecond) which uses Math.floorDiv(), so that the effective bucket capacity is slightly less than the intended gasPerSecond/GAS_SCALE_FACTOR, causing legitimate requests near the boundary to be incorrectly throttled and their records to be missing from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ThrottleManagerImpl is a singleton bean (no request scope), so that the gasLimitBucket, rateLimitBucket, and opcodeRateLimitBucket are shared across all concurrent requests, allowing a single attacker to exhaust shared buckets and cause other users' records to be missing from mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the RequestProperties.test() method increments the counter before checking filters, so that even requests that don't match any filter still increment the counter, causing the limit to be reached prematurely and disabling the filter for legitimate requests, leading to missing records in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the RequestProperties.test() counter is never reset (AtomicLong with no reset mechanism), so that after reaching the limit, the filter is permanently disabled for the lifetime of the application, causing all subsequent requests to bypass REJECT/THROTTLE actions and generate unthrottled records in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the BLOCK filter field uses ContractCallRequest::getBlock which returns a BlockType enum, converted to string via String.valueOf(), by crafting requests with specific BlockType values whose string representations match filter expressions, triggering unintended actions and causing records to be incorrectly handled in mirror node exports?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: throttle(ContractCallRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that throttle() does not atomically check-and-consume from both rateLimitBucket and gasLimitBucket, so that under high concurrency, a request can pass the rateLimitBucket check but fail the gasLimitBucket check, consuming a rate token without generating a record, causing the rate limit to be effectively lower than configured and legitimate records to be missing from mirror node exports?
